title: raphanus maritimus subsp. littoralis
intro: Raphanus maritimus subsp. littoralis, also known as sea radish or littoral radish, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, native to the coastal areas of Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties, although its use should be supervised by a healthcare professional due to potential interactions and side effects.
